TEXT.source: NTU Corpus stories for Atayal, story: AtaNr-moving_Takun

S.FORM: kala panqu'anay mu ku' gingamilan nku' nanavakis sunga'.
S.TRANSL: 我來敘述以前祖先的起源。
I'll describe the origins of our ancestors in the past.

S.FORM: tiku gingamilan ta' ka navakis sunga' ga minkahul kia papak ru minsa'a'utu' ta' kia kahavag ka ka' a'iani runi i taiwan.
S.TRANSL: 我們祖先的起源來自大霸尖山，原本住在台灣的人們都曾經聚集在那裏。
The origin of our ancestors in the past was from the Papak Mountain, and those who were originally here in Taiwan had all been aggregated there.

S.FORM: tuliq ru wahan na vayhu la wahan na vayhu ru siluhey ku papak ru.
S.TRANSL: 突然間，風災就來了，然後風災來了之後大霸尖山就倒塌了。
Suddenly, a typhoon came, and after that the Papak mountain collapsed.

S.FORM: masugagai kumiyum su naniqun kahavag ku itaal runi taiwan ru.
S.TRANSL: 原本住在台灣的人們就分開去尋找食物。
All the people originally living here in Taiwan then separated to look for food.

S.FORM: aruwa yumiax tigi karinku ku ruma' muwah tigi taoyuan ku ruma' muwah tigi vari ku ruma' musa nantou ku ruma'.
S.TRANSL: 有些人往下到了花蓮；有些人來到桃園；有些人來到苗栗；有些人去到了南投。
Some went downward to Hualien; some came to Taoyuan; some came to Miaoli; some went to Nantou.

S.FORM: suoyi tiku hani la ga ini' paska' kahavag na'aring kia ini' paska' ku kai' ta' ka itaal ru.
S.TRANSL: 所以現在大家彼此都不同了。自從那時候泰雅族的語言就都彼此不相像了。
So, now we are all different from each other. Our languages of the Atayal people have all become different since then.

S.FORM: tiku muvaq runi i vari hani ga taiyazu salun ku minuka muvaq vari ga aqih gi qaisun ragiax la ga ini' paska' ku hanaang na ka kai' la gi.
S.TRANSL: 下到苗栗這裡的，就稱做為泰雅族。但是因為被山脈隔開，語言的腔調就彼此不相像。
Those who came downward to Miaoli here are called the Atayal people. Since we are separated by the mountains from each other, unfortunately, the accents of our languages do not resemble each other.

S.FORM: vali asi nku zitai sku hani maglu karari'ax ru ru maska' ku kai’ kia ku ruma' imuliq ku hanaang na kai’ kia ku ruma' ga ga mahuay ku kai’ nha' ru.
S.TRANSL: 不像現在的時代每天聚集在一起，語言才會彼此相向。（因為被山脈隔開）有一些人語言的腔調比較重，有些人他們的語言腔調比較溫和謙遜。

It wasn't like the modern era when we gather around everyday, so that the languages resemble each other. (Because being separated by mountains,) there are some people with heavy accents, and there are those whose language sounds moderate.

S.FORM: kia ku san mha matu'au kia ku san mha matu'uwal.
S.TRANSL: 有些人（的語言）就稱作Matu'au，有些人（的語言）就稱做Matu'uwal。
There are those who are (whose language is) called Matu'au, and there are those who are (whose language is) called Matu'uwal.

S.FORM: ru' tiku sami ka mavatu'an hani hiya' ga na'aring sami i kia muwah ru ukas vay naniqun.
S.TRANSL: 而我們在Mavatu'an這裡，自從我們來了之後就真的都沒有食物。
As for us, the people of Mavatu'an here, since we had come, there really wasn't any food.

S.FORM: asi paglu kina qasinu gi kia ukas a patus sunga' gi vali kia a qavuli' uwi.
S.TRANSL: 只能和野獸在一起，因為以前沒有槍，也沒有火藥。
We had no choice but stay together with the beasts because there were no guns in the past, and no gun powder either.

S.FORM: nanak ku puting takis vay ku rasun mian ru.
S.TRANSL: 我們所攜帶的真的只有獵刀。
What we brought were really only the hunting knives.

S.FORM: iniwan na qasinu kinqiyanux mian sunga' i.
S.TRANSL: 我們過去的生活就和野獸一樣。
Our lives in the past were the same as those of the beasts.

S.FORM: talan qutux qasinu ga tutingun ru nanu yani ku siqiyanux nku sami ka itaal hani.
S.TRANSL: 如果看到一隻野獸，就把牠打死，我們這裡的泰雅族人就是靠這個維生。
If we saw a beast, we beat it, and that was what we, the Atayal people here, depended on when living.

S.FORM: ru maqahquwaw sami ru muwah sami i yahu na papak la ru.
S.TRANSL: 我們下坡然後就來到大霸尖山的下面。
We went downhill and came to the foot of the Papak Mountain.

S.FORM: ru lumuag tikay su gaghap na na pati' la ku itaal.
S.TRANSL: 泰雅人找到了一些稻米的種子。
The Atayal people found some rice seeds.

S.FORM: ki'i vinuway vay nku kamisama' ku yani su kaqiyanux ru.
S.TRANSL: 大概是上帝的賞賜，那樣才能過活。
That's probably God's gift, so that we can live on in that way.

S.FORM: ta'aringun kia tumuvux.
S.TRANSL: 從那時候開始就播種。
From then on we have sowed.

S.FORM: ikia tikay ku irarawq na kahuniq ga pinangun ru ru tuvuxan tikay vali kituwa' ska' suvalay tahunakan.
S.TRANSL: 如果有一些樹長得較矮的地方，就拿來開墾，然後播一點種，範圍不大，剛好可以坐下。
If there were some areas with short trees, we cultivated them, and sowed a little bit in some areas that were not large but were really just large enough for one to sit on.

S.FORM: ga musa' i qumaluap ku ku tamalung hiya' ru.
S.TRANSL: 男人們去打獵。
The men went hunting.

S.FORM: ukas a timu' sunga'.
S.TRANSL: 以前沒有鹽。
There was no salt in the past.

S.FORM: nanuan ku timu' sunga' pisa.
S.TRANSL: 以前的鹽巴是什麼呢?
What do we have as salt in the past?

S.FORM: tiku pa panaqali' su qali' na kahuniq ka tatimu' rakus ga isamama'ah tikay la ga yani a timu' ru XkitkuX pring ga vuway nia' ga alan su timu'.
S.TRANSL: 樹皮如果有鹹味，像是樟樹，如果有點酸酸的，那個就當作鹽巴。羅氏鹽膚木的果實也拿來當作鹽巴。
For the bark of trees that was salty, like that of the camphor trees, if it was a little sour, we made it into salt. For the Rhus javanica tree, its fruits were taken as salt.

S.FORM: ru maqaq muwah lawwi maqahquwaw lawwi ru puah mha vay i mavatu'an la.
S.TRANSL: 再往下坡就到了Mavatu'an。
As they came down hill a little more, they arrived at Mavatu'an.

S.FORM: ru muwah ku gipun la yani ku maymal su kaal ru masiya matupagal su kaal masiyaw larua? ku gipun hani ru.
S.TRANSL: 然後日本人就來了。日本人在這有超過六十年嗎?
And the Japanese came. Were the Japanese here over sixty years?

S.FORM: ki'i nia' talan ru kia suquliq ka suguhiloq i ragiax ru tu'un nia' ku gipun mha hali magan ru pasivaqay ta' su paptawwaw mha ku gipun ru.
S.TRANSL: 他們大概看到山上有人在生煙，就說："去帶他們！" 日本人就說："我們來教他們工作吧！"
They probably saw there were people making smoke in the mountains, and said "Go take them!" "Let's teach them how to work!" said the Japanese.

S.FORM: ru pasivaqan sami nia' su patwawan la ru.
S.TRANSL: 然後，他們便教導了我們工作的方式。
Then, they taught us the way of working.

S.FORM: kumaay sami su slaq la.
S.TRANSL: 我們就挖田。
We dug some fields.

S.FORM: ku yani ga tiku kinqiyanux mian sku kinaki mian i mavatu'an sunga' ki ga.
S.TRANSL: 這就是我們過去留在Mavatu'an的生活。
This is just our lives during our stay in Mavatu'an in the past.

S.FORM: mumuwa' su sayi' mumuwa' su qagirang nanu’ yani ku siqiyanux mian ru.
S.TRANSL: 種植芋頭，種植豆子，我們就是以此維生。
we planted taros and planted beans. That's what we depended on when we lived.

S.FORM: gini'un taal ga aqih gi qaat kuing i sinlanga'an sinvilan kai’ nku navakis sunga' ga.
S.TRANSL: 這麼看來，很不幸地我沒有跟隨過去老人家的交代。
Seen in this way, it is unfortunate that I did not follow the orders and the words of the elders in the past.

S.FORM: valayq uwi ku kinqiyanux ka asi nku yani ga gi ukas tikay a inakuxan.
S.TRANSL: 像那樣的生活也是很好，因為比較沒有擔心的事。
Living like that is also good, because there is less to worry about.

S.FORM: ini' kuxaal sunga'.
S.TRANSL: 過去沒有病痛。
There was no sickness in the past.

S.FORM: ni'i luwaw su kaqilan ru nanu’ asi kaqiyanux asi suvalay nku ku sqas qasinu.
S.TRANSL: 不會找到不好的事，而且能夠就只像野獸那樣生活。
One would not find terrible things, and could just live like the beasts.

S.FORM: panqu'anay simu ku na'aring i i mavatu'an ru taihuk sku savawqil hani ku rauwq na ralu' ralu' nku rauwq ka inuwahan mian.
S.TRANSL: 我來敘述從Mavatu'an開始一直到部落Savawqil這裡，以及我們所走過土地的地名。
I'll describe how we started from Mavatu'an and arrived at Savawqil here, and the names of the places which we passed through as we travelled.

S.FORM: matu'an tigi runi nia’ ga ga gawng kating XsernX.
S.TRANSL: 最往Mavatu'an 那一邊，是Gawng Kating.
Up toward Mavatu'an is Gawng Kating.

S.FORM: tigi runi nia' ga miyahu nia' lawwi ga miyahu nia' lawwi ga sayta' siri.
S.TRANSL: Mavatu'an 那一邊，但是Gawng Kating再下來一點，是Sayta' Siri。
Toward the side of Mavatu'an, but down from Gawng Kating is Sayta' Siri.

S.FORM: s say mayahu' nia' la ga takmaya'an.
S.TRANSL: Sayta' Siri的下面，是Takmaya'an。
Downward from Sayta' Siri would be Takmaya'an.

S.FORM: muwah la ga paqalapaw.
S.TRANSL: 再往這邊來，是Paqalapaw。
Coming further toward us from Takmaya'an, one would reach Paqalapaw.

S.FORM: miyahu nia' la ga tinapa'an.
S.TRANSL: Paqalapaw的下面就是Tinapa'an。
Down from Paqalapaw would be Tinapa'an.

S.FORM: ru yahu nia' la ga tatapihan.
S.TRANSL: Tinapa'an的下面是Tatapihan.
Down from Tinapa'an would be Tatapihan.

S.FORM: miyahu' nia' la ga nawmatah.
S.TRANSL: Tatapihan的下面就是Nawmatah。
Down from Tatapihan would be Nawmatah.

S.FORM: ru taihuk sugu'ivu' ru yahu' nku sugu'ivu' lawwi ga nanu’ yani ku salun mha savawqil la ru nanu’ hanian sami maki sku kaki'an mian hani ru.
S.TRANSL: 然後就到了Sugu'ivu'，然後Sugu'ivu'再往下就是我們所稱呼的Savawqil，也就是我們現在所在的地方。
Afterwards one would arrive at Sugu'ivu', and much downward from Sugu'ivu' would be what is called Savawqil, which is the place we are at right here now.

S.FORM: umiyu ku malahang ita' la.
S.TRANSL: 統治我們的人已經改變了。
Those ruling us have changed now.

S.FORM: sayugun nku tu'iya' la ku malahang ita' la ru.
S.TRANSL: 統治我們的人已經替換成漢人。
Those ruling us have been replaced by the Chinese.

S.FORM: vali i aqih la pi nage ki kinlahangan nku gipun ka tikuksiyugi ku.
S.TRANSL: 並不是說帝國主義日本統治的不好。
It's not that the imperialist Japanese treated us badly when ruling us.

S.FORM: ku kinlahangan na tu'iya’ hani ka si kavalayq la ru.
S.TRANSL: 漢人也統治的很好。
The Chinese ruled us well.

S.FORM: mahuway suvalay ku malahang ita' hani ka.
S.TRANSL: 非常感謝在這裡統治我們的人。
Thank those who are ruling us here.

S.FORM: anananu nian nia' i sipasivaq ru.
S.TRANSL: 不管如何，他們都在教導。
No matter what, they are teaching.

S.FORM: uN nian nia' i kinkiyu ku inkahulan nku navakis nku itaal.
S.TRANSL: 他們在研究泰雅族的起源。
They are doing research on the origin of the Atayal people.

S.FORM: ini' kusa m mha uw simu ka itaal ga ga simu nanak ini' kusa ru mahuway suvalay ru.
S.TRANSL: 他們並沒有說你們原住民單獨就好，真的很感謝。
They didn't say "You aborigines, live by yourself!" We are really thankful.

S.FORM: nanu' uw ita' ka itaal hani ki ga kalawkahay ta' kahavag naki ta' i ma manavalayq maqiyanux ku vavawuraw hani.
S.TRANSL: 我們這裡的原住民們，我們大家一起努力，才能好好的在這塊土地上生活。
We aborigines here, let's all work hard, so that we can live well on this land.

S.FORM: ana ku kamisama' uwi ga kalahangay ta' nia'.
S.TRANSL: 就連上帝也會照顧我們。
Even God will take care of us.

S.FORM: asi ta' paspungi kamisama' ka ma'usa' malahang ita'.
S.TRANSL: 我們就順從會照顧我們的耶穌吧。
Let's just obey God, who's going to take care of us.

S.FORM: mahuway simu.
S.TRANSL: 謝謝你們。
Thank you all.
